Art Studies · HCI / UX Research · Affective Interfaces · Language, Memory, and Public Systems

I build research-driven interfaces and participatory formats that examine how people interpret affect, navigate public systems, and make meaning through language, records, and interaction.

Current focus

Art-theoretical HCI research on affect, language, memory, and civic information systems.

Email
mueleesu@gmail.com

Profile

Undergraduate student in Art Studies at Hongik University building a research-and-design practice across art theory, language, affect, memory, and human-computer interaction. My work focuses on how people interpret emotions, experience systems, and make meaning through interfaces, participatory formats, and public records.

Education

Hongik University, College of Fine Arts

B.A. Candidate, Art Studies / Aesthetics · Seoul, Republic of Korea
  • Focus: art theory, aesthetics, visual culture, interaction, language, affect, public systems, and design research.

Research Interests

Human-Computer Interaction UX Research Interaction Design Affective Communication Emoji & Online Emotion Civic Information Systems Public-sector UX Memory & Archives Information Access Failure Experience Participatory Art Data Visualization

Service Experience

Republic of Korea Army

Remains Recovery TF · DNA-related Administrative Work · Military Service, ongoing
  • Supported administrative communication, family-contact records, archival document requests, verification procedures, and data organization related to Korean War remains recovery.
  • Developed a practical understanding of memory, bureaucracy, information access, archival procedures, and public-sector UX.
  • This experience informs current research on civic information systems, public records, interface failure, and state-led remembrance work.

Projects

Selected projects combine art-theoretical inquiry with interface design, survey methods, and exhibition-oriented prototyping.

Para-moji: Affective Contradiction in Text–Emoji Communication

Independent HCI / Art Research Project · 2026 – Present
  • Defined “Para-moji” as a text–emoji affective strategy in which emotionally incongruent combinations intensify rather than weaken affection, sincerity, intimacy, ambiguity, or relational tension.
  • Designed surveys and comparative interfaces to analyze how variants such as “I miss you,” “I miss you ♥,” “I miss youㅋㅋ,” and “I miss you😭” are interpreted across sincerity, burden, lightness, intimacy, playfulness, and flirtation.
  • Positioned the project within emoji pragmatics, computer-mediated communication, and text–emoji congruency/incongruency research, while differentiating it as affection-specific, amplification-oriented, culturally situated, and design-research oriented.
  • Conducted preliminary SNS observation and revised the research direction after finding that public comments often use a narrow set of conventional emojis; the project shifted from platform-based collection to situation-based interpretation.
  • Built an interactive bilingual prototype with selection, comparison, loading, and metric-detail states for research presentation and exhibition contexts.

The Call Room: Interface of Memory, Access, and Institutional Failure

Independent Interactive Design Research Project · 2025 – Present
  • Developed a web-based installation concept from field experience with remains recovery, public records, administrative workflows, and bereaved-family communication.
  • Explores the question: who becomes searchable, and what happens when a public system cannot explain why one record appears while another remains inaccessible?
  • Uses a search interface, incomplete results, error states, sound, darkness, and procedural uncertainty to make users experience the limits of record access.
  • Frames remembrance not only as mourning, but also as labor, infrastructure, access, bureaucracy, and procedural design.

Love Density Field / Tower of Words

Interactive Affective Visualization Prototype · 2026 – Present
  • Designed an interactive affective visualization that maps repeated Korean emotional expressions into visual density, color-coded affective scores, dominant-emotion states, print output, and layered sound.
  • Investigates how repeated phrases such as “보고싶다,” “사랑해,” and “고마워” accumulate into a field of emotional traces.
  • Connects survey-based affective variables with exhibition-oriented typography, sound mapping, and printable visual output.

Love Songs as Data

Exploratory Research Track · 2026 – Present
  • Developing a data-oriented research direction that treats Korean popular love songs and K-pop lyrics as cultural data rather than only sentimental content.
  • Investigates how love-language changes across time, vocal position, gendered address, and the globalization of K-pop through English lyrics.
  • Connects lyric analysis, affective language, text mining, and visualization as a broader context for the Para-moji project.

Korean Language Curriculum for International Art Students

Content-based Language Education / Cultural Mediation Project
  • Designed a content-based Korean language curriculum for international fine arts students, focusing on artistic self-expression, Korean cultural context, and communication within art-school environments.
  • Connected language learning with campus space, Hongdae culture, art-school communication, cultural adaptation, and career planning.
  • This project informed later interests in language, cultural mediation, education design, and user-centered communication.

Education & Facilitation

Korean Language Tutor for International Art Students

Content-based Korean Language Education · Hongik University
  • Taught Korean to international graduate students in painting using a content-based approach.
  • Focused on art-related vocabulary, self-expression, critique language, and communication in Korean art-school contexts.
  • Connected language learning with artistic identity, studio practice, and cultural adaptation.

Midam Art-Psychology Group

Co-founder / Program Organizer · Youth Art-Psychology Program in collaboration with Seoul Donghaeng
  • Co-founded an art-psychology group and developed youth-oriented art-psychology education programs in collaboration with Seoul Donghaeng.
  • Designed and facilitated art-psychology activities for adolescents, connecting creative expression, emotional reflection, and interpersonal communication.
  • This experience informs later interests in participatory art, emotional interaction, and community-based design.

Writing & Participatory Practice

Poetry and Literary Practice

  • Writes poetry as a core creative practice, with interests in urban life, memory, disappearance, death, solitude, and existential perception.
  • Recipient of multiple university-level literary awards in poetry, essays, and critical writing.
  • Expanding poetic thinking into spatial, interactive, and experiential formats beyond conventional written poetry.

Participatory Poetry Writing Performance

  • Conducted participatory writing sessions in which short poems were written for participants based on conversation, affect, and personal impressions.
  • This practice later informed interests in participatory art, language, and emotional interaction.
